creator: figyshki@gmail.com
text:
Environment:
Hardware: Clevo M570TU, integrated WiFi
CVSup'ed 03 Sept 2008 against RELENG_7 branch. Same behavior seen in 7-STABLE.

Cablevision's cable modem to PFsense 1.2-RELEASE access point. WPA-PSK in use. 30-char WPA passphrase.

/etc/wpa_supplicant.conf (sanitized):
ctrl_interface=/var/run/wpa_supplicant
ctrl_interface_group=0
ap_scan=1
fast_reauth=1
eapol_version=1

#
# WPA2 auth for here.
#
network={
        ssid="Moria"
        scan_ssid=1
        proto=WPA2
        key_mgmt=WPA-PSK
        psk=<EncodedStringHere>
}

wpi0: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
        ether 00:1c:bf:6c:39:f5
        inet 10.0.0.21 netmask 0xffffff00 broadcast 10.0.0.255
        media: IEEE 802.11 Wireless Ethernet autoselect (OFDM/54Mbps)
        status: associated
        ssid Moria channel 36 (5180 Mhz 11a) bssid 00:02:6f:4b:c6:ec
        authmode WPA2/802.11i privacy ON deftxkey UNDEF TKIP 2:128-bit
        TKIP 3:128-bit txpower 50 bmiss 7 scanvalid 60 roaming MANUAL

/etc/rc.conf has: 'fconfig_wpi0="WPA DHCP"'

Transfers crawl at speeds measured in bps, ~900 bps.

Fix: Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: Uncertain. Above-listed environment seems to do it, though.
